action_plan: Collect at least 7 days of DMARC aggregate reports before policy changes.
If fail rate is stable <=1%, move to p=quarantine with pct=25 for a staged rollout.
Increase pct to 100 once legitimate traffic impact is validated.
Move to p=reject after at least one stable reporting cycle.
Enable DKIM signing for each sender platform using your domain or aligned subdomain.
Validate DKIM selectors and key DNS records for each sending service.
Check forwarding/list transformations that may invalidate DKIM signatures.
Publish SPF on aligned envelope-from domains for each sending platform.
Confirm MAIL FROM domains are aligned to the visible header-from domain.
Audit envelope-from domains used by each sender and align them to your domain.
Update SPF include chains for all legitimate senders.
Prioritize investigation and remediation of the top failing source first.
Confirm whether that source is an approved sender, then fix or block it.
Prioritize DKIM alignment for legitimate sender paths because DKIM survives forwarding better than SPF.
Review override-reason traffic before treating all failures as malicious.
aggregate_evidence_note: DMARC aggregate reports are record-level summaries, not message-level traces.

anti_spoofing_posture_breakdown.detail: Policy pct does not reduce spoof-blocking coverage.
Monitor-only posture leaves failing traffic un-enforced.

attack_pressure_note: Attack pressure reflects unauthorized DMARC failures outside approved sender traffic.

deliverability_safety_breakdown.detail: 0.00% fail rate on traffic used for readiness.
No additional enforcing-policy delivery risk applied.
Low message volume lowers confidence in safe-enforcement conclusions.
Policy pct coverage does not reduce safety score.

deliverability_safety_note: No deliverability-safety penalty applied: observed failures are outside the readiness basis (approved_and_auto_approved_non_noise_senders) and do not impact currently approved sender traffic.

dns_diagnostics.reason: DNS diagnostics disabled for this run.

enforcement_readiness_detail: Only 25 messages in readiness basis (approved_and_auto_approved_non_noise_senders). Gather more data before policy changes.

health_score_breakdown.detail: Monitor-only policy (p=none) carries small score penalty.

health_score_causes: Historical trend score: DMARC fail rate cost 50.0 points: 26.47% of messages fail DMARC.
Historical trend score: Aligned auth best-path coverage cost 42.9 points: Best aligned method coverage is 73.53% (target >= 95%).
Historical trend score: Aligned auth blended coverage cost 28.9 points: Combined DKIM/SPF aligned coverage is 36.76% (target >= 85%).
health_score_summary: Historical Trend Score uses all observed traffic and subtracts points for DMARC failures, weak aligned authentication coverage, and monitor-only policy posture.
historical_trend_score_description: Historical Trend Score uses all observed traffic (including receiver-side noise) for long-term visibility. Deliverability Safety is the enforcement-risk metric.

actions: Publish SPF on aligned envelope-from domains for each sending platform.
Confirm MAIL FROM domains are aligned to the visible header-from domain.
issues.evidence: SPF auth pass count is zero.
issues.likely_cause: SPF records may be missing, broken, or not published for active sender envelope domains.
issues.title: No SPF authentication passes were observed.

actions: Prioritize investigation and remediation of the top failing source first.
Confirm whether that source is an approved sender, then fix or block it.
issues.evidence: 198.51.100.9 accounts for 100.0% of failing messages (9 of 9).
issues.likely_cause: A single sender platform or abuse source is driving most DMARC failures.
issues.title: Failures are concentrated on a narrow source set.

actions: If fail rate is stable <=1%, move to p=quarantine with pct=25 for a staged rollout.
Increase pct to 100 once legitimate traffic impact is validated.
Move to p=reject after at least one stable reporting cycle.
issues.likely_cause: Policy has not been moved into enforcement yet, usually due to unresolved sender coverage.
issues.title: Domain is still in monitor-only mode (p=none).

actions: Enable DKIM signing for each sender platform using your domain or aligned subdomain.
Validate DKIM selectors and key DNS records for each sending service.
Check forwarding/list transformations that may invalidate DKIM signatures.
issues.evidence: DKIM auth pass is 73.53%, but aligned DKIM is 73.53%.
issues.likely_cause: Some senders are not DKIM-signing with an aligned d= domain, or signatures are getting broken in transit.
issues.title: DKIM alignment coverage is low.

actions: Audit envelope-from domains used by each sender and align them to your domain.
Update SPF include chains for all legitimate senders.
issues.evidence: DKIM aligned pass is 73.53% vs SPF aligned 0.00% (SPF auth 0.00%).
issues.likely_cause: SPF may pass on non-aligned envelope domains while DKIM handles most aligned auth.
issues.title: SPF alignment trails DKIM.

actions: Collect at least 7 days of DMARC aggregate reports before policy changes.
issues.evidence: Only 34 messages observed; thresholds are less reliable.
issues.likely_cause: Short reporting window or low send volume.
issues.title: Limited report volume.

actions: Prioritize DKIM alignment for legitimate sender paths because DKIM survives forwarding better than SPF.
Review override-reason traffic before treating all failures as malicious.
issues.evidence: 25 messages include override reasons such as forwarded/trusted_forwarder/mailing_list/local_policy.
issues.likely_cause: Forwarding and intermediary handling can break SPF and occasionally DKIM, causing DMARC failures that are not pure spoofing.
issues.title: Forwarding/local-policy override signals were observed.

assumptions: Projection treats DMARC-failing volume as potentially affected under enforcing policy.
Actual mailbox outcomes vary by receiver local policy and forwarding behavior.

readiness_gate.detail: Only 25 messages in readiness basis (approved_and_auto_approved_non_noise_senders). Gather more data before policy changes.

recommendations: [high/legitimate_misconfiguration/low] No SPF authentication passes were observed. SPF auth pass count is zero.
[high/unauthorized_or_misconfigured_sources/low] Failures are concentrated on a narrow source set. 198.51.100.9 accounts for 100.0% of failing messages (9 of 9).
[medium/policy_posture/low] Domain is still in monitor-only mode (p=none). Published policy is mostly p=none (consistency 100%).
[medium/alignment_gap/low] DKIM alignment coverage is low. DKIM auth pass is 73.53%, but aligned DKIM is 73.53%.

sender_inventory.classification_reason: Observed sender has >=98% DMARC pass rate and meets auto-approval minimum volume (10 messages) in the current report window.

evidence_details.aggregate_record_notice: DMARC aggregate reports provide record-level buckets, not individual email events.
